response_text: This is false. Rust does not have a garbage collector. Instead, Rust uses a system of ownership with rules that the compiler checks at compile time to ensure memory safety. This system is based on concepts like ownership, borrowing, and lifetimes, which allow Rust to manage memory without needing a garbage collector. This approach enables Rust to offer high performance similar to languages like C and C++, while also providing safety guarantees that help prevent common bugs such as null pointer dereferences and buffer overflows.
